ddls:
CREATE TABLE "survival_data" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each observation */,
  "event_at" REAL /* Time to event or censoring */,
  "removed" INTEGER /* Number of events (e.g., failures) at this time point */,
  "observed" INTEGER /* Number of observations (e.g., subjects) at this time point */,
  "censored" INTEGER /* Number of censored observations at this time point */,
  "entrance" INTEGER /* Number of subjects entering the study at this time point */,
  "at_risk" INTEGER /* Number of subjects at risk (i.e., not yet failed or censored) at this time point */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the study or experiment */,
  "hazard" REAL /* Estimated hazard rate at this time point */,
  "covariate1" REAL /* First covariate for detailed analysis */,
  "covariate2" REAL /* Second covariate for detailed analysis */,
  "covariate3" TEXT /* Third covariate for detailed analysis */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of event (e.g., failure, churn, death) */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to the studies table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survival_data_study_id FOREIGN KEY ("study_id") REFERENCES studies ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "survival_models" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of survival model (e.g., Kaplan-Meier, Cox PH, Weibull) */,
  "param1" REAL /* First model parameter (e.g., shape, scale, beta) */,
  "param2" REAL /* Second model parameter (e.g., baseline hazard, frailty) */,
  "aic" REAL /* Akaike information criterion */,
  "bic" REAL /* Bayesian information criterion */,
  "log_likelihood" REAL /* Log-likelihood of the model */,
  "covariate1_coefficient" REAL /* Coefficient for covariate1 */,
  "covariate2_coefficient" REAL /* Coefficient for covariate2 */,
  "covariate3_coefficient" REAL /* Coefficient for covariate3 */,
  "study_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key linking to the studies table */,
  "model_version" TEXT /* Version of the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_survival_models_study_id FOREIGN KEY ("study_id") REFERENCES studies ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "studies" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each study */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the study */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the study */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the study */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the study */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the study was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)
column_names: Number of events (e.g., failures) at this time point
Number of observations (e.g., subjects) at this time point
Number of censored observations at this time point
Number of subjects entering the study at this time point
Number of subjects at risk (i.e., not yet failed or censored) at this time point
